title: rhus glabra var. elegans
intro: Rhus glabra var. elegans, also known as Smooth Sumac, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America, characterized by its glossy green leaves and red or purple berries. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Smooth Sumac include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cold and flu symptoms, and support the immune system, while also providing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of Smooth Sumac include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves, bark, or berries in hot water, and can be used to treat a range of health issues.
